relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Receive the tender conscience without turning welcome into argument.
Different trust-levels show up at the table.
The eater must not despise, and the abstainer must not judge.
Each servant stands before the Lord who can make them stand.
Days are discerned differently; each must be persuaded in mind.
Eating and abstaining can both become thanksgiving to the Lord.
No one lives or dies as an isolated self.
Living and dying belong to the Lord.
Messiah is Lord of dead and living.
Judgment and contempt of siblings collapse before the Divine judgment seat.
Every knee and tongue will answer to the Divine.
Each gives account for oneself.
Stop judging; choose not to place stumbling before another.
Nothing is common in itself, yet conscience can experience it as common.
Food must not grieve the one for whom Messiah died.
Do not let freedom become spoken of as harm.
The reign is justice, peace, and joy in holy Spirit, not food battles.
Serving Messiah this way pleases the Divine.
Pursue peace and mutual building.
Do not tear down Divine work for food.
Love can choose restraint to keep the sibling from stumbling.
Trust may be held before the Divine without forcing another.
What is not from trust fractures the conscience.

contemplative_note: Romans 14 is table spirituality: freedom restrained by love, conscience honored, and the reign known as justice, peace, and joy in holy Spirit.
